checks.name: Catalog Documentation
checks.description:
The storefront of your solution on dataminer.services.

Must include:
• Clear value proposition
• Key features (max 5)
• Use cases
• Prerequisites (version ranges, not pinned)
• 1–3 screenshots

Contact details and personal email are forbidden.

checks.name: Source README
checks.description:
Developer-oriented README at the repository root.

Must cover:
• Architecture overview
• Getting started
• Project structure
• Contribution guide

Distinct from the Catalog README — targets contributors and integrators, not end users.

checks.name: Release Notes
checks.description:
Every release must have release notes in plain language.

Notes should describe what changed and why it matters — not just a list of commits or ticket numbers.

Written for non-technical audiences.

checks.name: Storage / DOM Objects
checks.description:
DOM modules must be properly scoped — and no solution may directly access a DOM module owned by another solution.

• Use [SdmDomStorage] + [GenerateExposers]
• Module IDs namespaced to solution
  (e.g. infraops/assets, not just assets)
• Storage mappers (*.g.cs) committed
• Cross-solution data flows through the owning solution’s typed IApiHelper — never via direct DomHelper

checks.name: SDM Compliance
checks.description:
Standard Data Model (SDM) — 3-tier architecture:

• T1 Foundational: SdmObject<T> models + repository storage
• T2 Encapsulated: IApiHelper interface accepting IConnection
• T3 Federated: registered via SdmRegistrar

All tiers must pass. This is an existing architecture standard.

checks.name: Inter-Solution References
checks.description:
Cross-solution dependencies must flow through typed NuGet API helpers — never direct DOM access.

• Declare consumed SDM packages in Directory.Packages.props
• Dependency graph documented and free of cycles

This is the natural outcome of SDM T3 compliance, not a new requirement.

checks.name: Typed API Objects
checks.description:
The solution exposes an IApiHelper interface with typed repository properties — one per domain object.

• IBulkRepository<T> or IObservableRepository<T>
• Consumers work with typed objects without touching DOM directly

This is SDM T2 compliance at the API surface level.

docUrls.label: AI and UI Ready by Default

checks.name: GQI & User-Defined APIs
checks.description:
Full external consumability requires both:

• GQI ad-hoc data sources — expose objects to dashboards and low-code apps
• UDAPI endpoints — expose objects to external HTTP clients and AI agents

docUrls.label: AI and UI Ready by Default

checks.name: NuGet Devpack Published
checks.description:
The solution’s typed API helper (IApiHelper + models) must be published as a NuGet package.

Publish to the internal Skyline feed so other solutions can take a typed dependency without referencing source code.

Without a devpack, cross-solution consumption requires code duplication.

checks.name: Component Privacy
checks.description:
Only the main solution package may be public in the Catalog.

Mark all sub-components private in manifest.yml:
  registrationOptions:
    isHidden: true

Affects: individual scripts, add-ons, demo data, internal installers.

Sub-components appearing publicly clutter the Catalog.

checks.name: Naming Convention
checks.description:
All components follow Skyline naming conventions:

• Automation scripts: SOLCODE-TYPE-name (hyphen)
• Apps, dashboards, elements: SOLCODE name (space)

SOLCODE is a 3-letter uppercase code unique to the solution.

This is an existing published guideline.

docUrls.label: Naming Conventions for Catalog Item Components

checks.name: Automated Tests
checks.description:
Unit tests must cover core business logic:

• Use DomConnectionMock or repository stubs
• Run offline — no live DataMiner system needed
• CI runs tests on every PR

Fast, reliable feedback without infrastructure.

checks.name: CI/CD Pipeline
checks.description:
GitHub Actions pipeline must:

• Build on every PR
• Run all tests automatically
• Publish NuGet / DataMiner package on release tag

Main branch must be green. Standard Skyline GitHub guideline.

checks.name: Dependency Health
checks.description:
Keep NuGet dependencies healthy:

• No deprecated packages
• All packages up to date
• Consistent SDM package versions across solutions that interact

Version drift between solutions causes runtime conflicts.

checks.name: Health & Freshness
checks.description:
Signs of a healthy, maintained solution:

• Commits within the last 3 months
• Green CI pipeline
• Low open-issue count
• Regular releases

All data sourced from the GitHub API.

checks.name: Low-Code App
checks.description:
A DataMiner low-code app must be present and functional.

• Named after the solution (SOLNAME)
• Operators can use it without training
• Clear navigation, consistent layout
• Follows DataMiner style guide

checks.name: Demo / Mock Mode
checks.description:
Mock data injected through the typed API to evaluate the solution without a live system.

• Covers typical use cases
• Toggled via API — not hardcoded in scripts

Currently the highest-value gap across all solutions.

checks.name: LCA UX Audit
checks.description:
The DataMiner low-code app must pass a full UX audit:

• DataMiner style guide compliance
• Consistent spacing and typography
• Proper status chips
• Empty states and loading indicators

Run the dataminer-ux-audit agent for a scored report.

checks.name: Product Owner
checks.description:
A named Product Owner identified in .github/CODEOWNERS.

The PO is accountable for:
• Roadmap
• Backlog
• Release decisions

Not just technical delivery — clear product ownership per Skyline practice.

checks.name: Active Backlog
checks.description:
GitHub Issues or Projects used as an active backlog:

• Issues labeled and triaged within 2 weeks
• Milestones or Projects show roadmap
• No issues untouched > 90 days

The issue-triage agent helps maintain hygiene.

checks.name: Catalog Published
checks.description:
Published in dataminer.services Catalog as ‘Standard Solution’:

• Semantic versioning
• Main range tagged
• Regular releases

One-time publishing is not enough — active release cadence shows the solution is supported.
